exec_args.query: SELECT name FROM sqlite_master WHERE type='table';

args.query:
SELECT "Index",
 SUM(CASE WHEN "Close" > "Open" THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS up_days,
 SUM(CASE WHEN "Close" < "Open" THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS down_days,
 SUM(CASE WHEN "Close" = "Open" THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS flat_days
FROM index_trade
WHERE "Index" IN ('IXIC','NYA','GSPTSE') AND "Date" BETWEEN '2018-01-01' AND '2018-12-31'
GROUP BY "Index";

exec_args.sql:
SELECT "Index",
 SUM(CASE WHEN "Close" > "Open" THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS up_days,
 SUM(CASE WHEN "Close" < "Open" THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS down_days,
 SUM(CASE WHEN "Close" = "Open" THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS flat_days
FROM index_trade
WHERE "Index" IN ('IXIC','NYA','GSPTSE') AND "Date" BETWEEN '2018-01-01' AND '2018-12-31'
GROUP BY "Index";

result.preview:
"The North American indices with more up days than down days in 2018 were:

- IXIC (NASDAQ): 44 up days, 31 down days
- GSPTSE (S&P/TSX Composite): 44 up days, 41 down days

The NYA (NYSE Composite) had 36 up days and 42 down days, so it did not have more up than down days."

args.answer:
The North American indices with more up days than down days in 2018 were:

- IXIC (NASDAQ): 44 up days, 31 down days
- GSPTSE (S&P/TSX Composite): 44 up days, 41 down days

The NYA (NYSE Composite) had 36 up days and 42 down days, so it did not have more up than down days.
